signals.reasoning: BTC is stable but lacks a real positioning edge, so in a red macro backdrop it stays a benchmark hold rather than an actionable setup.
ETH is flat with low squeeze pressure and only mildly positive funding, which is not enough to justify fresh risk here.
SOL has improved relative stability but the mechanical edge is too weak to overcome the still-defensive macro regime.
TAO remains one of the cleaner shorts because price is still trending lower and the board keeps a bearish bias without any meaningful squeeze support.
CHILLGUY is extended after a very large 24-hour run, funding is positive, and execution quality is poor, so fading it is cleaner than chasing it.
MINA still screens as a valid squeeze-style long because funding is deeply negative, the mechanical board is fully bullish, and price is holding up despite the macro drag.
ZEREBRO has upside momentum but the spread and liquidity quality are too weak to support a high-quality fresh entry.
KAITO is still weak, but the negative funding now offsets some of the short thesis, making it less clean than TAO on the downside.
WLFI is sliding with a crowded positioning profile and high cascade risk, so despite negative funding it still looks vulnerable to further downside continuation.
TNSR remains the best long on the board because funding is deeply negative, squeeze risk is high, and the mechanical score keeps it in full trade territory.
DASH has strong recent momentum but the move already looks stretched and the edge is not clean enough to buy or fade aggressively at this level.
